title: Certification Decision tab is added after the Risk Summary Review tab
"Propose Decision" button is visible for user with SPECIFY_UPDATE_DOC_DECISION privilege
"Submit for Approval" button is visible for user with SUBMIT_FOR_APPROVAL privilege; disabled until Proposed Decision is set
Risk Summary section shows three cards: ITS Control Summary, SDL FCSR Summary, Data Protection and Privacy Summary
Unresolved Findings section is displayed under Risk Summary with columns: Finding ID, Control ID, Description, Recommendation, Severity, Closed Actions
In Certification Approval status, the Certification Decision tab must display a DOC Approvals section listing the assigned approvers.
description: DOC-CERT-006: In Certification Approval status, the Certification Decision tab must display a DOC Approvals section listing the assigned approvers.

title: "Send for Rework" button is visible for user with SEND_FOR_REWORK privilege; opens a popup with mandatory Comment field
Stage name in the DOC pipeline header is "Risk Summary Review" (not "Review Risk Summary")
Orange warning icon with "Proposed certification decision is not specified." tooltip is shown until a Proposed Decision is set
Saved Proposed Decision is displayed in the DOC header Certification Decision badge and on the My DOCs tab
DOC Signatures section appears during Certification Approval status with columns: Approver Name, Role, Signature, Comment
Control ID in Unresolved Findings table is clickable and navigates to Control Detail (read-only on Issue Certification stage)
Closed Actions value is clickable and opens a List of Actions popup on the Certification Decision tab
"Monitor Action Closure" stage: visible on Actions Closure DOC (Certified with Exception/Waiver); hidden on standard Controls Scoping DOC
On a Completed/Certified DOC, opening the Certification Decision tab must show no rows in the Unresolved Findings section.

title: Submitting the popup saves the decision; button changes to "Edit" for users with SPECIFY_UPDATE_DOC_DECISION privilege

Approver rows match the Proposed Decision: Certified → 1 row (BU Security Officer); Certified with Exception → 2 rows (BU Security Officer, BVP); Waiver → 3 rows (BU Security Officer, CISO, Senior BVP) — CPSO is not included
"Provide Signature" button is available for the respective approvers with the appropriate privilege (graceful skip when user lacks privilege)
Clicking "Submit for Approval" opens a confirmation popup (graceful: dismissed with Cancel without confirming submission)

Clicking "Propose Decision" opens the Propose Certification Decision popup with: Proposed Decision dropdown (mandatory), Validity End Date datepicker (mandatory, shown when Decision = Certified or Certified with Exception), Due Date for Actions Closure datepicker (mandatory, shown when Decision = Waiver), Comment (mandatory)
Provide Signature popup: Signature dropdown (Approved/Rejected) + mandatory Comment field
description: PIC-7882: Signature popup form with approval/rejection options

title: Selecting "Rejected" in Provide Signature popup shows orange warning about rejection impact
description: PIC-7883: Visual warning when approver selects rejection

title: Approval reset: updating Proposed Decision clears all previously collected approvals
description: PIC-7679: Changing the proposed decision resets the approval workflow

title: Cancel button visible for CANCEL_DIGITAL_OFFER_CERTIFICATION privilege during Issue Certification
description: PIC-7677: Cancel DOC button availability on Issue Certification stage

title: Revoke button shown only if Proposed Decision = Certified with Exception and user has REVOKE_DOC
description: PIC-7676: Conditional Revoke button based on decision type and privilege

title: No data section updates occur after DOC reaches Issue Certification stage
description: PIC-7681: Data sections frozen once DOC enters Issue Certification

title: When Proposed Decision = Certified with Exception or Waiver, the "Actions Closure" stage appears in the DOC header pipeline
Confirming Send for Rework updates DOC stage/status to "Risk Summary Review" (Review Risk Summary); stage is marked with an orange "!" icon and tooltip showing the rework justification
If any approver rejects the decision, DOC status reverts to "Decision Proposal"; orange "!" icon appears on DOC Approvals section with tooltip "The proposed certification decision has been rejected."
"Provide Signature" button is disabled for all other approvers after a rejection, with tooltip "The proposed certification decision has already been rejected by one of the approvers."
Risk Summary data (SDL FCSR and Data Protection) is not updated after DOC enters Issue Certification stage
"View History" link in DOC header opens the DOC History popup dialog
History grid shows columns: Date, User, Activity, Description
The history grid must contain at least one record. A DOC Creation event must exist from when the DOC was initiated.
description: DOC-HISTORY-003: The history grid must contain at least one record. A DOC Creation event must exist from when the DOC was initiated.

title: History popup shows Search field, Activity filter dropdown, Date Range picker, and Search/Reset buttons
Filtering by Activity type narrows the history entries to the selected type
The Activity filter dropdown must expose at least 5 selectable activity types in addition to the default "All" option, confirming the LOV is fully populated by the application.
description: DOC-HISTORY-006: The Activity filter dropdown must expose at least 5 selectable activity types in addition to the default "All" option, confirming the LOV is fully populated by the application.

title: The Date column in the history grid must contain a non-empty date-like value (digits present) in the first data row, confirming the application renders timestamps for history events.
description: DOC-HISTORY-007: The Date column in the history grid must contain a non-empty date-like value (digits present) in the first data row, confirming the application renders timestamps for history events.

title: Entering text in the Search field and clicking the Search button must narrow the visible history rows. Clicking Reset must restore the full list.
description: DOC-HISTORY-008: Entering text in the Search field and clicking the Search button must narrow the visible history rows. Clicking Reset must restore the full list.

title: Reset clears search text and restores the Activity dropdown to its default state
Applying a DOC History date range must narrow the grid to rows whose Date values fall within the selected day.
description: DOC-HISTORY-010: Applying a DOC History date range must narrow the grid to rows whose Date values fall within the selected day.

title: DOC History pagination must support per-page selection and moving to the next page when more than 10 records exist.
description: DOC-HISTORY-011: DOC History pagination must support per-page selection and moving to the next page when more than 10 records exist.

title: Activity filter dropdown lists all 15 activity types (Action Plan Update, Certification Decision Update, DOC Creation, Details Update, DOC Cancellation, DOC Completion, DOC Revocation, DOC Stage Update, DOC Status Update, ITS Checklist Update, Risk Assessment Update, Roles Update, Send DOC for Rework, Summary Risk Review Update)

Pagination with per-page selector (10/20/30/50/100) works correctly for history records

"Start ITS Risk Assessment" button is disabled when mandatory Roles & Responsibilities fields are not set; hovering shows "Provide all required data marked by orange dots to proceed" tooltip

Risk Summary tab is frozen: FCSR Decision, PCC Decision, and Overall Risk Assessment cannot be changed

title: The "Cancel DOC" button must be visible in the DOC Detail header for a user with CANCEL_DIGITAL_OFFER_CERTIFICATION privilege on a Controls Scoping DOC.

title: Clicking the "Cancel DOC" button must open a confirmation dialog. The dialog must show a reason textbox and a confirmation button. This test dismisses the dialog to avoid mutating QA data.
description: TC-LIFECYCLE-002: Clicking the "Cancel DOC" button must open a confirmation dialog. The dialog must show a reason textbox and a confirmation button. This test dismisses the dialog to avoid mutating QA data.

title: On a Completed DOC (DOC 273), check whether the "Revoke DOC" button is accessible. This requires REVOKE_DOC privilege (DOC Lead / BU Security Officer). If the current test user lacks the privilege the button will be absent — the test skips gracefully so the suite is not broken. This is a non-destructive visibility-only check.
description: TC-LIFECYCLE-013: On a Completed DOC (DOC 273), check whether the "Revoke DOC" button is accessible. This requires REVOKE_DOC privilege (DOC Lead / BU Security Officer). If the current test user lacks the privilege the button will be absent — the test skips gracefully so the suite is not broken. This is a non-destructive visibility-only check.

title: Completing the Initiate DOC stage (filling details and submitting) advances DOC to Scope ITS Controls
Clicking "Start ITS Risk Assessment" advances DOC stage/status to "Risk Assessment"; all control statuses are set to "Evidence required"
Risk Assessment tab is available on the Risk Assessment stage and allows inputting risk values per control
"Add Controls" button remains available for user with SCOPE_IT_SECURITY_CONTROLS privilege on the Risk Assessment stage
Completing Risk Summary Review stage (Submit to DRL Review → DRL submits to Issue Certification) advances DOC to Issue Certification (Decision Proposal status)
Completing Issue Certification (all approvals provided) marks DOC as Completed with appropriate Certification Decision
When Proposed Decision = Certified with Exception or Waiver, Submit for Actions Closure button becomes available after all approvals
Submit for Actions Closure button is disabled until all approvers have provided approval; tooltip shows "DOC can be moved to Actions Closure when all required approvals are provided"
Revoking a Completed DOC changes DOC status to "Revoked"
After DOC is created (pending initiation), a "Provide DOC Details" task appears in My Tasks for the DO Team (IT Owner, PM, PO, Security Manager)
After DOC is created (pending initiation), an "Initiate DOC" task appears in My Tasks for DOCL
Both "Provide DOC Details" and "Initiate DOC" tasks are closed when DOC is initiated or cancelled
After DOC is initiated (Scope ITS Controls stage), a "Define controls scope for DOC" task appears in My Tasks for DOCL; closed when DOC moves to Risk Assessment or is cancelled
After Risk Assessment is launched, a "Provide control\'s details for ITS Risk Assessment" task appears for DO Team; closed when DOC moves to Risk Summary Review or is cancelled

After a control is first submitted for Risk Assessment (status → Under Review), an "Evaluate control submitted to ITS Risk Assessment" task appears for DOCL; closed when DOC moves to Risk Summary Review or is cancelled
After DOC is submitted to Risk Summary Review, a "Review completed ITS Risk Assessment" task appears for Digital Risk Lead; closed on moving to Issue Certification, rework, or cancellation
After DOC is returned for rework to Risk Assessment, "Update the ITS Risk Assessment data" tasks appear for DO Team and DOCL; closed on moving to Risk Summary Review or cancellation
After DOC moves to Issue Certification (Decision Proposal status), a task is created for Digital Risk Lead; closed on move to Certification Approval, rework to Risk Summary Review, or cancellation

After DOC moves to Issue Certification (Certification Approval, Certified decision), "Provide signature for DOC Decision" task appears for BU Security Officer; closed on DOC completion, proposal update, or revocation

After DOC is returned for proposal update (Decision Proposal status), an "Update DOC Decision Proposal" task appears for Digital Risk Lead; closed on move to Certification Approval, rework, or cancellation

After DOC is returned to Risk Summary Review (rework), "Update the ITS Risk Summary" task appears for Digital Risk Lead; closed on move to Issue Certification, rework to Risk Assessment, or cancellation
When a role is changed (Product Owner or PM updated on Product Details), the task for the previous user is closed and a new task is created for the new user
After DOC moves to Actions Closure stage, "Close unresolved findings and actions" task appears for DO Team and DOCL
Users listed in Roles & Responsibilities are the only recipients of DOC tasks and notifications; when a role is re-assigned, the new user takes over tasks and the old user\'s tasks are closed
Validate task creation and closure across full DOC lifecycle
description: PIC-8071: End-to-end task lifecycle validation for all DOC stage transitions

description: PIC-7625: Tasks assigned to correct users when DOC events fire

description: PIC-7621: Each notification type has correct email template configured

title: Verify dynamic placeholder replacement in actual triggered email
description: PIC-7622: Placeholders replaced with actual values in sent emails

title: Verify email and task trigger on role reassignment

title: Cancel DOC popup shows mandatory Comment field; Cancel DOC button disabled until comment provided
description: PIC-6168: Cancel DOC confirmation requires mandatory justification comment

title: Auto-created DOC: Release field auto-populated and disabled when triggered by release creation

title: Auto-created DOC: DOC Reason auto-filled from BackOffice DOC Reasons for Automatic Triggers
description: PIC-6236: DOC Reason field auto-populated from BackOffice configuration

title: My DOCs tab is visible on the Landing Page only for users with VIEW_DOC (linked to a DOC role) or VIEW_ALL_DOCS privilege; not shown for other users
My DOCs tab is positioned after the My Releases tab
My DOCs grid displays all expected columns: DOC Name, Product, VESTA ID, DOC Status, Certification Decision, Target Release Date, Created By, IT Owner, DOC Lead
DOC Name column contains clickable links that navigate to the DOC Detail page
Certification Decision column shows "–" until a decision is provided
DOC Status dropdown filter applies correctly
Reset button clears all active filters and restores the full DOC list
Pagination per-page selector (10/20/30/50/100) changes the number of rows displayed
Search field filters grid by DOC name
All grid columns are sortable; default sort is by DOC Name column
All My DOCs grid columns are sortable; clicking a column header re-sorts the grid
Product dropdown filter lists products with Digital Offer = Yes and filters the grid
Certification Decision dropdown filter filters DOCs by decision value
VESTA ID searchable dropdown supports type-to-search and filters the grid
DOC Lead user lookup filter narrows results to DOCs linked to the selected DOCL
"No Digital Offer Certifications to show" empty state message appears when filter returns no results
My Products table shows VESTA ID, Security Advisor, DOC Lead columns when ≥1 product has Digital Offer
description: PIC-8770: DOC-related columns dynamically shown in My Products table

title: My Products DOC columns hidden when all products have Digital Offer = No
description: PIC-8771: DOC columns removed from My Products when no DO products exist

title: My Products VESTA ID cell shows first 2 VESTA IDs with "+N" indicator for additional ones
description: PIC-8772: Truncated VESTA ID display with hover to reveal full list

title: Breadcrumb shows Home > Product Name > DOC: DOC Name with clickable links
DOC stage pipeline is visible with all 5 stages (Initiate DOC → Scope ITS Controls → Risk Assessment → Risk Summary Review → Issue Certification)
Current active DOC stage is highlighted in the pipeline
"Hide Flow" toggle hides the workflow pipeline; "Show Flow" restores it
Status badge displays the current DOC status (Pending Initiation, Controls Scoping, Completed, etc.)
The DOC ID displayed in the header must match the DOC-NNN format.
description: DOC-DETAIL-006: The DOC ID displayed in the header must match the DOC-NNN format.

title: "Digital Offer Details" tab is available after DOC initiation
"Roles & Responsibilities" tab is available after DOC initiation
"ITS Checklist" tab is available after DOC initiation
Completed workflow stages show the user who completed them and the completion date
For completed DOCs, Certification Decision badge (e.g., CERTIFIED) is visible in the header
Release link in header navigates to the associated Release Detail page
DOC Detail page header shows correct DOC Name, DOC ID, and VESTA ID
Cancelled DOC header shows "Cancelled" badge with info icon; hover tooltip shows cancellation reason
description: PIC-6168: Cancelled DOC displays status with info icon tooltip showing reason

title: DOC header shows 6 tabs in correct order: DO Details, Roles, ITS Checklist, Action Plan, Risk Summary, Cert Decision
description: PIC-6166: Verify tab ordering matches specification after DOC initiation

title: Overdue warning icon displayed in header for Waiver decision when due date ≤ 30 days remaining

description: TC-LIFECYCLE-006: After a DOC is Completed/Certified, the Digital Offer Details tab must be in read-only mode — the "Edit Details" button must NOT be present, ensuring the DOC Name, DOC Reason, and other fields cannot be changed.

title: After a DOC is Completed, the VESTA ID must remain visible in read-only mode and the Digital Offer Details tab must not expose any editable VESTA input control.

title: Roles & Responsibilities tab displays a grid with columns: User Role, Team Members, Email, Location
Grid shows the product-derived roles and, when assigned in the environment, additional workflow roles such as BU Security Officer, CISO, DOCL, Digital Risk Lead, BVP, and Senior BVP
Roles pre-populated from Product Details (read-only within DOC): IT Owner, Project Manager, Product Owner, Security Advisor / Security and Data Protection Advisor
"Edit Roles" button is visible for a user with EDIT_USERS_ROLES_DOC privilege
In edit mode, the Roles tab enters edit state and validates required-role completion before save
"Save Changes" button is disabled and shows tooltip "Specify all required members for their roles to save changes" if mandatory roles are empty
Cancel button in edit mode discards changes and returns to read-only view
An orange dot is shown on the Roles & Responsibilities tab name when any mandatory editable role has no member assigned
Unassigned editable roles show "No member assigned" in Team Members, "–" in Email and Location
Roles editable within DOC (for user with EDIT_USERS_ROLES_DOC privilege): BU Security Officer, DOCL, Digital Risk Lead, CISO, BVP, Senior BVP — edit mode shows user lookup input fields
The Roles & Responsibilities grid must display column headers
description: DOC-ROLES-011: The Roles & Responsibilities grid must display column headers: User Role, Team Members, Email, Location.

title: Saving with all mandatory roles populated (or Cancel) returns tab to read-only view with updated data
User with VIEW_DOC privilege can view Roles tab but cannot edit (no "Edit Roles" button)
description: PIC-6829: Read-only access for VIEW_DOC users on Roles & Responsibilities tab

title: After a DOC is Completed, the Roles & Responsibilities tab must be frozen — the "Edit Roles" button must NOT appear so that role assignments cannot be changed.
description: TC-LIFECYCLE-009: After a DOC is Completed, the Roles & Responsibilities tab must be frozen — the "Edit Roles" button must NOT appear so that role assignments cannot be changed.

title: For CPSO/CISO roles, a Deputy user can be selected in place of the principal
User with VIEW_DOC privilege can view the Roles & Responsibilities tab but cannot edit it (no "Edit Roles" button shown)
Control ID column contains clickable links navigating to the Control Detail page
ITS Checklist tab displays the "IT SECURITY CONTROLS" subtitle and a grid with columns: Control ID, Description, Evidence Expectation, Category, Actions
ITS Checklist grid must show columns
description: DOC-ITS-002: ITS Checklist grid must show columns: Control ID, Description, Evidence Expectation, Category (and an Actions column).

title: Category filter dropdown lists available Control Categories and filters the grid
"Add Control" button is visible for user with SCOPE_IT_SECURITY_CONTROLS privilege, but on the current QA DOC it is disabled because all available controls are already in scope
Clicking "Add Control" opens the Add Control(s) popup with Search, Category filter, "Show new only" toggle, and a controls table (Control ID, Description, Category columns with checkboxes)
Selecting controls in the popup shows "N of M Selected" count at the bottom; "Add Selected" button is disabled until at least one is selected
Search field filters controls by Control ID, Description, and Evidence Expectation text
Reset button clears all search filters
Actions column shows an X (Descope) button for user with SCOPE_IT_SECURITY_CONTROLS privilege; hovering shows "Descope Control" tooltip
Clicking Descope (X) opens the "Unscope ITS Control" popup with mandatory Justification field; Descope button is disabled until justification is provided
Control ID column is sortable
Description and Category columns are sortable
Clicking the Category column header in the ITS Checklist grid must re-sort the rows without breaking the grid layout.
description: DOC-ITS-011: Clicking the Category column header in the ITS Checklist grid must re-sort the rows without breaking the grid layout.

title: "No results found" empty state appears when filters return no matching controls
By default all active controls from BackOffice are loaded and sorted by Control ID for a newly created DOC
"No results found" empty state in Add Control popup when no filters match; if controls are already selected, count and enabled Add Selected button remain
Category filter dropdown narrows the ITS grid to the selected category; Reset restores the full list
Category filter in the Add Control popup narrows the controls list to the selected category
Selecting controls in the Add Control popup and clicking "Add Selected" must append those controls to the ITS Checklist table. The added control can then be descoped. Deferred
description: DOC-ITS-017: Selecting controls in the Add Control popup and clicking "Add Selected" must append those controls to the ITS Checklist table. The added control can then be descoped. Deferred: requires a dedicated DOC to avoid mutating shared QA seed data.

title: "Start ITS Risk Assessment" button is visible on a Controls Scoping DOC; button is disabled when any control has 'Not Assessed' status
Clicking "Start ITS Risk Assessment" on a fully-assessed DOC must advance the DOC stage to "Risk Assessment" and make the ITS Checklist read-only. Deferred
description: DOC-ITS-019: Clicking "Start ITS Risk Assessment" on a fully-assessed DOC must advance the DOC stage to "Risk Assessment" and make the ITS Checklist read-only. Deferred: permanently advances the DOC stage — requires a dedicated DOC that is safe to advance.

title: Verify all active controls loaded by default and sorted by Control ID for new DOC
description: PIC-6905: Default state shows all active controls from BackOffice sorted by Control ID

title: User with VIEW_DOC privilege sees ITS Checklist in read-only mode (no Add/Descope buttons)
description: PIC-6907: VIEW_DOC users cannot add or descope controls

title: Verify "Start ITS Risk Assessment" button is disabled when mandatory R&R fields are not set
description: PIC-6879: Button disabled with tooltip when Roles & Responsibilities has incomplete mandatory roles

title: After a DOC is Completed, the ITS Checklist tab must be frozen — the "Add Controls" button must NOT appear, ensuring control scope cannot be changed.
description: TC-LIFECYCLE-007: After a DOC is Completed, the ITS Checklist tab must be frozen — the "Add Controls" button must NOT appear, ensuring control scope cannot be changed.

title: After a DOC is Completed, the ITS Checklist tab must be fully frozen. The Descope (×) action link must NOT be present on any control row, preventing any further changes to the control scope.
description: TC-LIFECYCLE-012: After a DOC is Completed, the ITS Checklist tab must be fully frozen. The Descope (×) action link must NOT be present on any control row, preventing any further changes to the control scope.

title: Lazy loading is implemented — additional controls load as the user scrolls down
"No ITS Controls added yet" empty state with Add Control button appears when no controls are in scope
"No active ITS Controls for this product — refer to the PICASso support team" message appears when BackOffice has no active controls
Adding selected controls appends them to the ITS Checklist table
Previously descoped controls appear greyed in the Add Control popup with a tooltip icon showing the descope justification
Confirming descope removes the control from the ITS Checklist table
User with VIEW_DOC privilege can view the ITS Checklist tab in read-only mode (no Add Control or Descope buttons)
ITS Checklist shows additional columns during Risk Assessment: Status, Risk Level, Findings

description: PIC-7174: Control resubmission flow after clarification

description: PIC-7016: Submit gating rule for findings coverage

title: Header shows Control ID
Control Detail page is reachable from the ITS Checklist Control ID link and shows breadcrumb context for Home, Product, and DOC
Description and Evidence Expectation sections show full content
On Scope ITS Controls stage, a "No evidence links, findings or comments yet. Refer to this section once the DOC has been submitted to the Risk Assessment stage." message is shown
"Descope Control" button is visible on Control Detail for user with SCOPE_IT_SECURITY_CONTROLS privilege; triggers the same Unscope popup as from ITS Checklist
Header shows the current assessment status badge
Category label and Risk Level badge with justification text are displayed correctly
FINDINGS section lists control findings or shows "No findings added yet" when empty (Risk Assessment stage and beyond)
EVIDENCE LINKS grid shows attached evidence with clickable link URLs and link names
COMMENTS section displays a timeline of comments with date/time, user avatar, and message text
On a Completed DOC (DOC 273), the Control Detail page must be read-only
description: DOC-CONTROL-012: On a Completed DOC (DOC 273), the Control Detail page must be read-only: the "Descope Control" button must NOT be visible.

title: On a later-stage DOC control (Actions Closure), the Control Detail page must display a Risk Level label indicating the assessed risk level (e.g. High, Medium, Low).
description: DOC-CONTROL-013: On a later-stage DOC control (Actions Closure), the Control Detail page must display a Risk Level label indicating the assessed risk level (e.g. High, Medium, Low).

title: On a later-stage DOC control, the EVIDENCE LINKS section must either show attached evidence entries with clickable link URLs, or show a "No evidence links" empty state. When evidence exists, links must have valid href attributes.
description: DOC-CONTROL-014: On a later-stage DOC control, the EVIDENCE LINKS section must either show attached evidence entries with clickable link URLs, or show a "No evidence links" empty state. When evidence exists, links must have valid href attributes.

title: On a later-stage DOC control, the COMMENTS section must either show at least one comment timeline item (with date/time, user, and message text), or show a "No comments" empty state.
description: DOC-CONTROL-015: On a later-stage DOC control, the COMMENTS section must either show at least one comment timeline item (with date/time, user, and message text), or show a "No comments" empty state.

title: On Issue Certification stage (Completed DOC), Control Detail is read-only: no Descope, no Add Evidence, no comment textarea

"Add Action" button visible for user with CREATE_UPDATE_DOC_ACTIONS privilege

title: Action Name link opens Action Details view with Name, Status, Due Date, Assignee, Description
